name: Setup guide with EOL Python, wrong package name, and OS assumption

planted_issues.description: Guide instructs users to install Python 3.8, which reached end-of-life in October 2024. It no longer receives security patches. The project should require Python 3.11+ or at minimum 3.10+.

Step 3 installs `flask-auth` which is an abandoned/wrong package. The correct package for Flask authentication is `Flask-Login` (or `Flask-Security-Too`). Installing the wrong package will either fail or introduce an untrusted dependency.
The guide uses `brew install` (macOS Homebrew) without stating that macOS is required. Linux and Windows developers will encounter an error at step 2 with no guidance on what to do instead.

clean_areas.description: The 'Clone the Repository' section is correct and standard — proper SSH URL, cd into the directory.
